text.title: fco-cost-allocation Core Rules
text.summary: Migrated testable rules for fco-cost-allocation. Verify and refine before relying on for production.

rule.statement: All cloud resources MUST be tagged with environment, team, service, and cost-center at creation time. Resource creation MUST be blocked via SCP (AWS), Azure Policy, or GCP Organization Policy if any required tag is absent.
rule.rationale: Retroactive tagging achieves at best 60-70% coverage; some resource types cannot be tagged after creation. Enforcement at creation time is the only mechanism that achieves 98%+ compliance. Warning-based approaches without blocking achieve 40-60% compliance at best.

rule.statement: Measure untagged resource percentage weekly and report to engineering leadership. The target is below 5% of resources untagged. If the weekly measurement shows the percentage is stable above 5%, escalate the enforcement mechanism from warning-only to blocking.
rule.rationale: Weekly measurement creates accountability pressure. If the metric is not reviewed and acted upon, it becomes meaningless data. The 5% target is achievable with proper enforcement and represents the minimum threshold for meaningful cost attribution.
